title: How Do I Want to Relate to Money as a Partner?
subtitle: A journal prompt set for redesigning your relationship with money from moral judgement to intentional partnership
excerpt: A five-prompt journal set from Day 67 of a 12-week money program. The prompts explore the moral assumptions underlying your relationship with money, trace where they came from, and build a picture of the relationship you want to have instead.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Day 67 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you notice judgement about money or wealthy people, when having more feels morally uncomfortable, or when you want to consciously redesign how you think about your financial relationship.

content.text: A five-prompt journal set from Day 67 of a 12-week money program. Day 67 theme: money is a neutral tool shaped by values and intention. These prompts trace the moral assumptions you carry about wealth, find where they came from, and invite you to consciously design the relationship you want to have with money instead. Set a timer. Write without editing.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
What is the first word that comes to mind when I think of wealthy people?
Write it without censoring. It tells you something about your current frame.
What messages about money did I grow up with? Who passed them on?
Write the specific phrases or images that come up. The ones that still play in the background.
Where do I still judge myself (or others) for their relationship with money?
Write specific examples. The judgement is information, not proof.
If money is neutral and takes its character from the person who holds it, what character do I bring to it?
Write what you actually bring, not what you wish you did.
How do I want to relate to money going forward? Describe the relationship in one sentence.
Write the sentence you'd stand behind publicly. That's the frame you're building.
Adapted from the Day 67 theme of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
